response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack and systems developer with strong low-latency and event-driven experience, but almost entirely in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than C++. His only substantial C++ work was a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a university thesis, and C++ is absent from his current skills list. The role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise plus industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443, SDL), MQTT/IoT, and automation-domain knowledge, none of which appear on this resume, so the core requirements are not met despite his general seniority.
strengths: Strong systems-level experience: low-latency, asynchronous I/O, multi-threaded services handling millions of requests per second
Experience with event-driven architectures, networking, and message-based platforms across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Proven mentorship and knowledge-sharing record (workshops, courses, coding dojos) matching the role's mentoring expectations
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ experience (~7 months in 2014-2015); recent decade is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No industrial cybersecurity, SDL, or IEC 62443 exposure, and no MQTT, OPC UA, or automation-protocol experience
No industrial automation or IoT domain background; career centered on web/SaaS products

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 experience
Transferable multi-threading, networking, and event-driven systems skills
